title: Cuphea phoenix
taxonomy: Cuphea phoenix is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the genus Cuphea, which contains around 260 species of plants. The Lythraceae family, also known as the loosestrife family, comprises about 630 species of flowering plants. These plants are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Cuphea phoenix is native to the Americas and is often grown for its vibrant flowers and ability to attract pollinators.
uses: Cuphea phoenix is known for its potential use in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin American countries. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various health issues. Traditionally, Cuphea phoenix is used to treat conditions such as fever, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Cuphea phoenix in modern medicine.

intro: Cuphea phoenix, also known as Cuphea phœnice, is a plant species belonging to the Lythraceae family, commonly used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits.
